title: git-cliff configuration
description:
Configuration for git-cliff, a changelog generator that follows Conventional Commits.
https://git-cliff.org/docs/configuration/

$schema.description: Optional schema URL for editor integrations.

header.description: Header template rendered at the beginning of the changelog.

body.description: Body template rendered for each release.

footer.description: Footer template rendered at the end of the changelog.

trim.description: Whether to remove leading and trailing whitespace from the changelog body.

render_always.description: Whether to render the body template even if there are no releases to process.

postprocessors.description: Regex-based processors applied after changelog rendering.

output.description: Output file path for the changelog.

conventional_commits.description: Whether to parse commits according to the Conventional Commits specification.

require_conventional.description: Whether all included commits must be conventional.

filter_unconventional.description: Whether to exclude commits that are not conventional.

split_commits.description: Whether each line of a commit should be processed as an individual commit message.

commit_preprocessors.description: Regex-based processors applied before commit parsing.

commit_parsers.description: Commit parser rules that classify, scope, or skip commits.

protect_breaking_commits.description: Whether breaking changes are protected from being skipped by commit parsers.

link_parsers.description: Regex-based parsers that extract links from commit messages.

filter_commits.description: Whether commits not matched by a commit parser are filtered out.

fail_on_unmatched_commit.description: Whether git-cliff should fail when an included commit is not matched by any parser.

tag_pattern.description: Rust regex pattern for selecting release tags.

skip_tags.description: Rust regex pattern for tags to skip.

ignore_tags.description: Rust regex pattern for tags to ignore.

count_tags.description: Rust regex pattern for tags to count.

use_branch_tags.description: Whether to include only tags that belong to the current branch.

topo_order.description: Whether releases are processed in topological order instead of chronological order.

topo_order_commits.description: Whether commits are processed in topological order instead of chronological order.

sort_commits.description: Ordering for commits inside each release section.

limit_commits.description: Maximum number of commits included in the generated changelog.

recurse_submodules.description: Whether to read and process submodule commits.

include_paths.description: Glob patterns for paths whose commits should be included.

exclude_paths.description: Glob patterns for paths whose commits should be excluded.

offline.description: Whether to disable external calls even when remotes are configured.

native_tls.description: Whether to use the platform native certificate store.

features_always_bump_minor.description: Whether feature commits always trigger a minor version bump.

breaking_always_bump_major.description: Whether breaking changes always trigger a major version bump.

initial_tag.description: Initial version used when no tags are found.

custom_major_increment_regex.description: Rust regex pattern for custom commit types that trigger major increments.

custom_minor_increment_regex.description: Rust regex pattern for custom commit types that trigger minor increments.

no_increment_regex.description: Rust regex pattern for commit types that do not increment the version.

pattern.description: Rust regex pattern to match text.

replace_command.description: Command used to transform the matched text.

message.description: Rust regex pattern matched against the commit message.

body.description: Rust regex pattern matched against the commit body.

footer.description: Rust regex pattern matched against the commit footer.

group.description: Changelog group assigned to matching commits.

default_scope.description: Default scope assigned when a matching commit has no scope.

pattern.description: Rust regex pattern matched against the configured field.
Rust regex pattern used to find links in commit messages.

text.description: Optional text template for generated links.
